case_title: MARK A. BROWN VS. CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD et al
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER MARK BROWN'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF ADMINSTRATIVE MANDATE IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 24, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER MARK BROWN'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF ADMINSTRATIVE MANDATE IS GRANTED. RESPONDENTS ARE ORDERED TO AWARD PETITIONER THE UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE BENEFITS WITHHELD PLUS INTEREST ON THOSE BENEFITS. PETITIONER DID NOT ENGAGE IN MISCONDUCT TO DISQUALIFY HIM FOR UNEMPLOYMENT COMPENSATION BENEFITS. (UMEMP. INS. CODE 1256; AMADOR V. UNEMPLOYMENT INS. APPEALS BD. (1984) 35 CAL.3D 671, 678.) THE INSTANT ACTION IS DISTINGUISHABLE FROM MCRAE V. CUIAB, WHERE THE PETITIONER REFUSED TO FOLLOW AN EMPLOYER POLICY. (MCCRAE V. CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INS. APPEALS BD. (1973) 30 CAL.APP.3D 89.) HERE, PETITIONER ATTEMPTED TO CORRECT HIS MISTAKE BY GOING HOME TO CHANGE SHIRTS. (AR00024, AR00044, AR00065 - AR00066.) LASTLY, PETITIONER WAS TERMINATED ON THE FIRST INSTANCE OF PETITIONER'S VIOLATION OF THE EMPLOYER'S UNIFORM POLICY AFTER RECEIPT OF THE CUSTOM SIZED UNIFORM SHIRTS. (ROBLES V. EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPT. (2012) 207 CAL.APP.4TH 1029, 1035-36. SEE AR00030.) EDD'S REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER MARK BROWN'S MOTION FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEYS FEES. PETITIONER MARK A. BROWN'S MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF REASONABLE ATTORNEYS' FEES UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1021.5 IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNTS AND FOR THE REASONS SUMMARIZED BELOW. THE CRITERIA FOR A SECTION 1021.5 AWARD HAVE BEEN SATISFIED. THE LITIGATION RESULTED IN THE ENFORCEMENT OF AN IMPORTANT RIGHT AFFECTING THE PUBLIC INTEREST, CONFERRED A SIGNIFICANT BENEFIT ON THE GENERAL PUBLIC OR A LARGE CLASS OF PERSONS, AND THE NECESSITY AND FINANCIAL BURDEN OF PRIVATE ENFORCEMENT ARE SUCH AS TO MAKE THE AWARD APPROPRIATE. (CODE CIV. PROC. 1021.5.) THE COURT OF APPEAL'S HOLDING IN ITS PUBLISHED OPINION THAT FORMER EMPLOYEES ARE ENTITLED TO PREJUDGMENT INTEREST ON WRONGLY DENIED UNEMPLOYMENT BENEFITS AT THE RATE OF 10 PERCENT FURTHERS THE PUBLIC INTEREST IN THE PROMPT PAYMENT OF BENEFITS, WHICH THAT COURT RECOGNIZED IS "CRUCIAL TO THE UNEMPLOYMENT COMPENSATION INSURANCE SYSTEM." (BROWN V. CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INS. APPEALS BD. (2018) 20 CAL.APP.5TH 1107, 1118; SEE ALSO AMERICAN FEDERATION OF LABOR V. EMPLOYMENT DEVELOPMENT DEPT. (1979) 88 CAL.APP.3D 811, 821-22 ["THE UPHOLDING OF UNEMPLOYED WORKERS' RIGHT TO PROMPT PAYMENTS UNDER THE SOCIAL SECURITY ACT IS A SIGNIFICANT BENEFIT TO BOTH UNEMPLOYED WORKERS AND THE PUBLIC AT LARGE."]. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING) COUNSEL FOR PETITIONER IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312 AND EMAIL IT TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG .JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM =(302/HEK)

calendar.matters:
Motion For Reasonable Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jan-29-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gary S. Garfinkle, Esq., Telephone 925-932-3737, Richard M. Pearl, Esq. Telephone 510-546-5074, who is also present, appearing for petitiioner;
Dane C. Barca, PhD, Deputy Attorney General Department of Justice State of California, Telephone 415-510-3371, appearing for Defendant Califoria Unemployment Insurnace Appeals;

Court confirms with counsel the receipt of the the tentive ruling that was emailed to all parites on January 28, 2018 due to the length of todays tentative ruling. All parties confirm receipt of the tentative ruling.

Ruling - A R G U E D: The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER MARK BROWN's Motion For Reasonable Attorneys Fees.

Petitioner Mark A. Brown's motion for an award of reasonable attorneys' fees under Code of Civil Procedure section 1021.5 is granted in the amounts and for the reasons summarized below.

The criteria for a section 1021.5 award have been satisfied. The litigation resulted in the enforcement of an important right affecting the public interest, conferred a significant benefit on the general public or a large class of persons, and the necessity and financial burden of private enforcement are such as to make the award appropriate. (Code Civ. Proc. 1021.5.) The Court of Appeal's holding in its published opinion that former employees are entitled to prejudgment interest on wrongly denied unemployment benefits at the rate of 10 percent furthers the public interest in the prompt payment of benefits, which that court recognized is "crucial to the unemployment compensation insurance system." (Brown v. California Unemployment Ins. Appeals Bd. (2018) 20 Cal.App.5th 1107, 1118; see also American Federation of Labor v. Employment Development Dept. (1979) 88 Cal.App.3d 811, 821-22 ["the upholding of unemployed workers' right to prompt payments under the Social Security Act is a significant benefit to both unemployed workers and the public at large."].

(SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING)

Counsel for Petitioner is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312 and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org .

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com =(302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion To Further Enforce Writ On Remand From Court Of Appeal
Off Calendar Jul-19-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Gary Garfinkle, Esq. 925-932-3737 for PETITIONER MARK BROWN.
Dane Barca, Esq. Deputy Attorney General 415-510-3371 of the Office of the Attorney General for Respondent.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER MARK BROWN's Motion To Further Enforce Writ of Mandate on Remand From Court Of Appeal.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Petitioner Mark Brown's motion to further enforce writ of mandate on remand from the Court of Appeal is denied. The only issues raised by Mr. Brown's motion are the appropriate rate for postjudgment interest and whether there is delayed accrual of postjudgment interest. The Court of Appeal addressed both issues and this court is not at liberty to reach a different decision. Even if this court had the authority to reach a different decision than the Court of Appeal, this court declines to do so out of respect for the considered decision of the Court of Appeal. The Court of Appeal stated: "Pursuant to subdivisions (a) and (c) of section 965.5 of the Government Code, interest on a 'judgment for the payment of money against the state or a state agency' commences to accrue '180 days from the date of the final judgment or settlement.' The rate is the constitutional rate of 7 percent." (Brown v. California Unemployment Ins. Appeals Bd. (2018) 20 Cal.App.5th 1107, 1120). There is nothing equivocal or uncertain about the quoted language. None of the exceptions to the law of the case doctrine applies.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Vicki Gross, CSR #5525, vgross@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/HK)

Amended Notice Of Motion For Enforcement Of Writ Of Administrative Mandate And Related Relief
Off Calendar Apr-14-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. GARY S. GARFINKLE, Tel No. (925) 932-3737 appearing for Petitioner Mark A. Brown;
Attys. BEVERLY R. MEYERS of the Office of the Attorney General, Tel No. (415) 703-5545, and JAMES HERING of the Employment Development Department, Tel No. (916) 654-7792 appearing for Respondents California Unemployment Insurance Appeals Board and Employment Development Department.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER MARK BROWN'S Amended Motion For Enforcement Of Writ Of Administrative Mandate And Related Relief is granted in part and denied in part. Respondents did not reasonably comply with the Court's May 23, 2013 Order and Writ of Administrative Mandamus. The Order and Writ required Respondents to "immediately" pay Petitioner his past-due unemployment benefits and to meet and confer with Petitioner's counsel about the method of payment. It was not reasonably compliant with the Order and Writ to instead unilaterally demand Petitioner retroactively fill out bi-weekly certifications for the entire period before Respondents paid him the past-due benefits, or to refuse to actually meet and confer with Petitioner's attorney as the order required. The Court finds this failure to comply was without good cause or substantial justification, and therefore levies a $1000 fine against Respondents, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1097. However, Respondents are correct that the interest due on the benefits should have been paid at 7% per annum, not 10% as Petitioner alleges. Unemployment benefits are not a contractual obligation. (See Rivera v. Patino (N. D. Cal 1981) 524 F.Supp. 136, 143-44.) As such, the generic interest rate on judgments applies, rather than the 10% rate for breach of contract; Respondents overpaid, rather than underpaid, interest due to Petitioner. Petitioner is entitled to reasonable attorney's fees under Government Code section 800 according to proof. Respondent shall immediately pay any unpaid amount to Petitioner less overpayments, if any. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Adminstrativ Mandate
Off Calendar May-23-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gary S. Garfinkle, Esq. and Maria J. Garfinkle, Esq. of the Law Offices of Gary Garfinkle, Tel. 925.932.3737, for petitioner and moving party;
Elizabeth Edwards, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 415.703.5536, for respondent and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER MARK BROWN's Motion For Writ Of Adminstrative Mandate is Granted. Respondents are ordered to award Petitioner the unemployment insurance benefits withheld plus interest on those benefits. Petitioner did not engage in misconduct to disqualify him for unemployment compensation benefits. (Umemp. Ins. Code 1256; Amador v. Unemployment Ins. Appeals Bd. (1984) 35 Cal.3d 671, 678.) The instant action is distinguishable from McRae v. CUIAB, where the petitioner refused to follow an employer policy. (McCrae v. California Unemployment Ins. Appeals Bd. (1973) 30 Cal.App.3d 89.) Here, Petitioner attempted to correct his mistake by going home to change shirts. (AR00024, AR00044, AR00065 - AR00066.) Lastly, Petitioner was terminated on the first instance of Petitioner's violation of the employer's uniform policy after receipt of the custom sized uniform shirts. (Robles v. Employment Development Dept. (2012) 207 Cal.App.4th 1029, 1035-36. See AR00030.) EDD's request for dismissal is denied. Order signed in open court. Peremptory Writ of Mandate signed in open court. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
